behavior_summary: U8P_TAPESTNS::look is a short U8P_TAPESTNS::look/inspect handler that displays a single player-facing bark 'tapestry' and then suspends the process.
The routine contains no conditionals, menus, flag reads/writes, or external gameplay logic beyond presenting the text and pausing.
This is a purely descriptive handler for a tapestry object — it does not change game state or call other game systems.
notable_control_flow: Linear: display bark text then suspend/pause the process.
No branching, loops, or flag-based guards are present.
Appears to be triggered by the engine's U8P_TAPESTNS::look/inspect process (processType 0x0208); may also run on fast-area entry but that is inferred.
open_questions: Is U8P_TAPESTNS::look invoked only by the explicit player 'U8P_TAPESTNS::look' command, or also by entering a fast-area tied to the tapestry?
Does the suspend node merely yield to the engine (brief pause), or does it wait for player dismissal/confirmation?
Are there other related scripts that provide a longer tapestry description under different conditions (not present in this file)?

main_function.name: U8P_TAPESTNS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspect/description response for the tapestry object: show the text 'tapestry' to the player and suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game's 'U8P_TAPESTNS::look'/inspect action on the TAPESTNS object (canonical: processType 0x0208).
Likely (inferred) also runnable when the player enters a U8P_TAPESTNS::look-type fast-area associated with the object.

function_map.name: U8P_TAPESTNS::look
function_map.role: Short U8P_TAPESTNS::look/inspect handler that emits a single bark and then suspends; no state changes.

external_calls.purpose: Pause or yield the process immediately after the bark is shown.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_TAPESTNS::look is a short U8P_TAPESTNS::look/inspect handler that displays a single player-facing bark 'tapestry' and then suspends the process.
